title: Tighten weak test assertions in test_costs_page.py
analysis: This task fixes 8 weak/misleading assertions in test_costs_page.py. All changes are test-only — no production code modifications needed. Each fix replaces a vague assertion (e.g. checking for '$' anywhere, checking '<' in HTML, checking a single occurrence) with a precise assertion that validates the actual expected rendered output. The test file already passes (110 tests); tightened assertions should also pass since the underlying templates render correct data. Key observations: (1) costs.html has exactly 7 sections with morph:innerHTML and Alpine.store hx-vals patterns, (2) summary.html renders total_spend as '$17.34' from mock data (12.34 + 5.00), (3) error fallback renders 'No cost data available for this window.', (4) Alpine store init is `Alpine.store('costs', { window: "7d" })`, (5) by_account.html renders spend as '$9.50' and '$3.20', (6) by_role.html embeds role names via tojson and Chart.js labels, (7) runs.html renders null task_id as em-dash and null title as 'unassigned'.

prerequisites.description: Verify all 110 existing tests pass as baseline before making changes

steps.description: Tighten test_default_window_7d_marked_active: replace `assert '7d' in html` with assertion that Alpine.store init contains '7d' as the default window value — check for `Alpine.store('costs', { window: "7d" })` in the rendered HTML
Verify step-1 passes: the costs.html template renders `Alpine.store('costs', { window: "7d" })` when no ?window= param is provided

Tighten test_summary_shows_fallback_content_on_error: replace `assert '<' in html` with `assert 'No cost data available for this window.' in html` — this is the exact fallback text from summary.html's {% else %} branch when summary is empty
Verify step-3 passes: when get_cost_summary raises, the handler sets summary={}, and the template renders the empty-data fallback message

Tighten test_hx_vals_count: add assertion that `Alpine.store("costs").window` (or single-quote variant) appears exactly 7 times — one per section (summary, by-project, by-account, by-role, trend, events, runs). Keep existing old_pattern_count == 0 assertion
Verify step-5 passes: costs.html has exactly 7 hx-vals expressions each containing Alpine.store("costs").window
Tighten test_sections_use_morph_swap: replace `assert 'morph:innerHTML' in html` with `assert html.count('morph:innerHTML') == 7` to verify all 7 polling sections use morph swap, not just one
Verify step-7 passes: costs.html has exactly 7 `hx-swap="morph:innerHTML"` attributes

Tighten test_renders_total_spend: replace `assert '$' in html` with `assert '$17.34' in html` — the computed value from mock data (dark_factory: 12.34 + other_project: 5.00 = 17.34, formatted via '%.2f')
Verify step-9 passes: summary.html aggregates total_spend across projects and formats as $17.34

Tighten test_handles_null_task_id: add content assertions beyond status_code — fetch HTML and assert the em-dash placeholder appears for null task_id (the template renders '—' when task.task_id is None) and assert 'unassigned' appears for null title
Verify step-11 passes: runs.html renders '—' for null task_id and 'unassigned' for null title

Tighten TestCostsByAccountPartial: add a new test method test_renders_spend_values that asserts '$9.50' and '$3.20' appear in the rendered HTML — the exact spend values from _MOCK_BY_ACCOUNT formatted via '%.2f'
Verify step-13 passes: by_account.html renders spend as '${{ "%.2f" | format(data.spend) }}' which produces '$9.50' and '$3.20' from mock data

Tighten test_role_names_appear_in_output: add assertion for 'reviewer' in html alongside existing 'implementer' check — both role names from _MOCK_BY_ROLE should appear in the rendered Chart.js config via tojson
Verify step-15 passes: by_role.html embeds by_role via tojson which includes both 'implementer' and 'reviewer' keys, and Chart.js labels include roleNames from Object.keys()

design_decisions.decision: Assert Alpine.store init pattern for 7d default rather than aria-pressed or CSS class
design_decisions.rationale: The costs.html template uses Alpine.store('costs', { window: "7d" }) for state management. This is the authoritative source of the default window. Asserting on the store init is more robust than checking button CSS classes which depend on Alpine runtime evaluation.

design_decisions.decision: Assert exact fallback text string rather than HTML structure
design_decisions.rationale: The summary.html template has a single, specific fallback message: 'No cost data available for this window.' Asserting this exact text is far more meaningful than '<' in html (which is trivially true for any HTML response) and will catch regressions in the fallback UX.

design_decisions.decision: Assert exact counts (7) for morph:innerHTML and Alpine.store hx-vals patterns
design_decisions.rationale: There are exactly 7 polling sections in costs.html. Counting ensures all sections have the pattern, not just one. If a section is added without the pattern, the count will fail. If a section is removed without updating tests, the count will also fail.

design_decisions.decision: Assert computed dollar values from mock data rather than just '$' presence
design_decisions.rationale: Asserting '$17.34' for total spend and '$9.50'/'$3.20' for account spend validates the template's aggregation logic and formatting. Just checking for '$' would pass even if the template was broken and rendered a static dollar sign.

design_decisions.decision: Add content assertions for null task_id test rather than replacing status_code check
design_decisions.rationale: Keep the existing status_code == 200 assertion (verifying no crash) and add content assertions for the em-dash placeholder and 'unassigned' text. This tests both resilience and correct rendering.

design_decisions.decision: Add a new test method for account spend values rather than expanding existing test
design_decisions.rationale: TestCostsByAccountPartial already has focused tests for specific concerns (chart, table, badges). Adding a separate test_renders_spend_values method follows the existing pattern of one concern per test.

reuse.how: The combined total_spend (12.34 + 5.00 = 17.34) drives the tighter assertion in test_renders_total_spend
The spend values (9.50, 3.20) drive the new test_renders_spend_values assertions
Contains both 'implementer' and 'reviewer' role keys that should appear in rendered output

reuse.what: _MOCK_RUNS data fixture with null task_id entry
reuse.how: Second task entry has task_id=None and title=None; template renders '—' and 'unassigned' fallbacks
All tightened tests reuse the existing patch helpers unchanged — no new mocking infrastructure needed
Starlette TestClient wrapping dashboard app — all tests already use this
